name: Animal Crossing: New Horizons
description: Ah yes, everyone's lockdown game of 2020. It's hard to overstate how big of a thing this was during the pandemic, but if you never picked it up, there's still plenty of reasons to play now. They added free DLC twice since launch, and a paid expansion for Happy Home Designer. If you loved it on the DS you'll love it here too. It only requires a small amount of time each day, but I've not see anyone put this game down in under 100 hours.

name: Advance Wars 1+2 Re-Boot Camp
description: The Advance Wars I've played the most is Dual Strike, but even though these two games are technically simpler by comparison, there is SO much content here. I came to appreciate the simplicity as elegance, and ended up with over 150 hours played. There's no Advance Wars inspired game on the Switch that I think can beat this.

name: Astral Chain
description: This was directed by the game designer for Nier: Automata, under supervision from the creator of Devil May Cry and Bayonetta. If that means something to you then perhaps this game is for you? It has a unque central mechanic of having a chain between you and your other character, and you tie the chain round things in combat. The combat is fast and has a lot of variety, and it's not a super long game either, but personally it didn't stick with me much. Not really my style of gameplay.

name: Bravely Default II
description: I loved the first Bravely Default, and at times I liked this game too. But by the end I was so sick of it I was just seeing what cheese strategies I could use to roll credits as fast as possible. I think there's still some interesting things going on here, and it mixes up the traditional turn based combat plus jobs system you see in so many of these games, but I'm not sure I recommend it unless you're a huge fan of these kind of games.

name: Cadence of Hyrule
description: This is Crypt of the Necrodancer, but Zelda. But it also leans way heavier into Zelda than I expected. You basically have a randomly generated Zelda overworld to explore, with shuffled items and dungeon floors - this is something no other Zelda game has done. It's difficult for sure, but the music is great, and the general execution very good too.

name: Crash Team Racing Nitro-Fueled
description: I have good memories of playing this back on the PS1, although I'll admit the visuals have not aged well. This game looks much better, and it feels more skill based than a Mario Kart, but it runs awfully, and the load times between levels are actually painful. It's hard to recommend as a multiplayer game, but it does have a more robust single player than Mario Kart, so if you can stand the frame rate and load times, you might have some fun.

name: Dragon Quest Builders 2
description: I've been recommending this game ever since I first played it, and I still cannot say enough good things about it. But now I have one extra thing to say - it's Pokopia but before Pokopia came out. It's an exceptionally generous game, there's so much to do, so much depth to the mechanics. I liked it so much I bought it at completed it on two different platforms.

name: Ori and the Will of the Wisps
Shin Megami Tensei III Nocturne HD Remaster
Story of Seasons: Friends of Mineral Town
Story of Seasons: A Wonderful Life
Story of Seasons: Pioneers of Olive Town
The Legend of Zelda: Tears of the Kingdom
NEO: The World Ends With You
The World Ends With You: Final Remix
Xenoblade Chronicles 2: Torna – The Golden Country
The Legend of Zelda: Echoes of Wisdom
Harvest Moon The Windos of Anthos
Kirby Air Riders
description: Riders at the ready! Pick your rider, pick your machine, and mount up for competition! Take on your rivals in frantic arena battles or fast-paced races on the ground and in the air. Use the Boost Charge button to brake and control your turns as your machine automatically fights for top speed. Fill your Boost Charge gauge as you drift around the bend and release it to trigger an explosive dash!

name: Rhythm Paradise Groove
description: You can do anything with rhythm! No matter how strange the situation, the rules are always the same: just press buttons in time with the beat! Hop through hoops, catch flying vegetables, swing sledgehammers, and much more – perform surprising feats with the music as your guide!

name: Rune Factory: Guardians of Azuma
description: Choose from one of two protagonists whose destinies are intertwined by mysterious forces. Use sacred treasures to fight against the Blight’s many forms to restore the people, and even gods, to their natural glory. Take farming further, as you’ll manage more than a plot, rebuilding entire villages and cultivating new allies and resources along the way. Explore seasonal-themed villages heavily inspired by traditional Japanese culture, each with brand-new festivals and revamped fan-favourites, as well as charming romance candidates.
